items.label: Rotation (Vr)
items.value: 59 KIAS
items.note: Apply AFM technique and runway-specific briefing.

items.label: Best rate climb (Vy)
items.value: 74 KIAS
items.note: Use AFM/POH for weight and altitude corrections.

items.label: Best angle climb (Vx)
items.value: 65 KIAS
items.note: Obstacle context dependent; verify AFM table.

items.label: Best glide
items.value: 75 KIAS

items.label: Performance chart caution
items.value: Use conservative margins for grass/wet/contaminated runway.
items.note: AFM reference required for dispatch decisions.

items.label: Takeoff roll
items.value: Full power available, verify engine indications in green and acceleration trend
items.note: Reject if expected power/acceleration is not achieved.

items.label: Initial climb (straight ahead)
items.value: Full climb power, hold AFM climb speed band (typical training 70-75 KIAS)

items.label: 30 degree right-bank climbing turn
items.value: Maintain full climb power, coordinated rudder, and about 72-78 KIAS
items.note: Do not sacrifice speed margin while turning low level.

items.label: Obstacle departure segment
items.value: Use obstacle-climb profile first, then transition to normal climb speed

items.label: Climb to cruise transition
items.value: At level-off, reduce power smoothly and retrim during acceleration

items.label: Normal cruise
items.value: Set RPM in AFM cruise band and cross-check temps/pressures regularly

items.label: Economy cruise
items.value: Lower cruise RPM setting from AFM with expected lower TAS and fuel flow
items.note: Use when schedule allows and cooling remains acceptable.

items.label: Turbulence handling
items.value: Hold moderate power and target turbulence penetration speed discipline

items.label: Descent entry
items.value: Reduce power progressively and trim for planned descent speed
items.note: Avoid abrupt idle dives when not required.

items.label: Circuit downwind abeam threshold
items.value: First pattern reduction, pitch for approach trend, trim and verify spacing

items.label: Base leg
items.value: Second reduction as needed to hold target base speed and glide geometry

items.label: Final approach (flapless)
items.value: Slightly higher target speed with moderate power support
items.note: Expect longer float; brief runway use.

items.label: Final approach (landing flap)
items.value: Lower target speed band with fine power corrections for glidepath

items.label: Go-around from final
items.value: Smooth full power, directional control, positive climb, staged flap retraction

items.label: Go-around from banked base-to-final
items.value: Full power, reduce bank toward wings-level, then establish climb speed
items.note: Do not combine high bank + high pitch + low speed.

items.label: If manifold pressure gauge is fitted
items.value: Use full available MP for takeoff/climb and table-based MP/RPM pairs in cruise
items.note: Reference AFM engine limitations and instrument arc markings.

items.label: Carb heat power effect (if icing conditions apply)
items.value: Expect temporary RPM drop with heat on; adjust power and retrim accordingly

items.label: Low on final correction
items.value: Add a small amount of power first while holding target speed attitude

items.label: High on final correction
items.value: Reduce power and verify stabilized profile gates

items.label: Energy rule of thumb
items.value: Pitch primarily controls speed, power primarily controls glidepath
items.note: Use together, not as isolated controls.

items.label: Data confidence
items.value: All numbers here are training quick-reference, not dispatch authority

items.label: Situation: 30 degree climbing turn after departure
items.value: Power: full climb; Speed: about 72-78 KIAS; Config: clean; Focus: coordination and stall margin
items.note: Requested scenario card; verify with AFM and instructor profile.

items.label: Situation: 20 degree noise-abatement climbing turn
items.value: Power: climb setting; Speed: normal climb band; Bank: shallow and coordinated
items.note: Delay any power reduction until procedure altitude.

items.label: Situation: level-off to cruise
items.value: Power: smoothly reduce to cruise; Speed: accelerate then trim to target TAS

items.label: Situation: joining downwind high/fast
items.value: Power: early reduction; Speed: re-capture pattern band before abeam

items.label: Situation: base leg with tailwind effect
items.value: Power: reduce earlier; Speed: protect base target; Geometry: avoid overshoot
items.note: Go around instead of forcing steep corrections.

items.label: Situation: final low but stable
items.value: Power: add slightly; Pitch: hold speed attitude; Goal: shallow glidepath recovery

items.label: Situation: final high but stable
items.value: Power: reduce; Speed: hold target; Config: only within AFM limits
items.note: If unstable by gate, go around.

items.label: Situation: balked landing from flare
items.value: Power: full; Attitude: arrest sink then climb; Config: staged cleanup

items.label: Situation: cruise descent planning from high altitude
items.value: Power: step down smoothly; Speed: hold descent target; Cooling: avoid abrupt drops

items.label: Situation: MP-equipped aircraft cruise set
items.value: Use AFM MP/RPM pair for altitude and OAT, then trim and monitor temps/pressures
items.note: Do not use one-size-fits-all manifold pressure numbers.

items.label: Situation: 30 degree climbing turnout with speed bleed
items.value: Keep climb power, reduce pitch slightly, and shallow bank until speed recovers
items.note: Protect stall margin before perfect track.

items.label: Situation: downwind high and fast
items.value: Make early moderate power reduction, re-trim, and recover profile before base

items.label: Situation: overshooting base-to-final
items.value: Prioritize safety; if unstable, execute go-around with full power and cleanup sequence
items.note: Do not force steep low-speed correction.

items.label: Situation: bounce and uncertain landing continuation
items.value: Transition immediately to go-around profile: power, attitude, stabilize climb, then cleanup

items.label: Situation: late descent planning from cruise
items.value: Use controlled higher descent rate within limits and keep speed discipline
items.note: Never chase profile with abrupt power/attitude changes.

items.label: Situation: turbulence in cruise
items.value: Use moderate stable power and penetration-speed discipline over schedule speed

items.label: Situation: high engine temperatures in prolonged climb
items.value: Adjust climb profile for cooling airflow and monitor trends continuously

items.label: Situation: MP-equipped climb at altitude
items.value: Expect lower available MP with altitude even full throttle; fly performance accordingly

items.label: Power Settings - Circuit
items.value: Downwind/base/final profile control, speed stability, small power corrections.

items.label: Power Settings - Climb Turns
items.value: Departure climb, banked climb turns, obstacle transition, climb cooling management.
items.note: Includes the 30 degree right-bank scenario set.

items.label: Power Settings - Go Around
items.value: Short-final go-around, balked landing, bounced touchdown recovery, flap cleanup timing.

items.label: Power Settings - MP Equipped
items.value: Manifold pressure behavior with altitude and approved MP/RPM table usage.

items.label: Power Settings - Cruise & Descent
items.value: Cruise stabilization, turbulence power handling, descent entry and energy control.

items.label: Power Settings - Fundamentals
items.value: Core scan pattern: set power, set attitude, trim, verify, then refine.

items.label: Power Settings - Nightmare Mode
items.value: High-workload trap scenarios combining speed decay, track pressure, gusts, and late decisions.
items.note: Go-around triggers and stability gates are core.

items.label: Power Settings - Nightmare Mode 2
items.value: Near-miss scenarios where multiple answers look plausible and only one best protects margin.
items.note: Focus on tie-breaker logic under pressure.

sections.title: Nightmare Mode - Trap Patterns and Saves

items.label: Trap: climbing turn + speed decay + heading pressure
items.value: Unload pitch slightly, reduce bank if needed, keep climb power, re-stabilize before precise track.
items.note: Speed margin outranks cosmetic track correction.

items.label: Trap: overshoot base-to-final with gusts
items.value: If not stable by gate, go around immediately with full power and standard sequence.
items.note: Never force steep/skidding correction close to stall margin.

items.label: Trap: low + slow + distracted on final
items.value: Aviate first: recover energy profile with power and attitude, then communicate.

items.label: Trap: bounce with marginal runway remaining
items.value: Commit to go-around profile immediately: full power, directional control, positive climb, staged cleanup.
items.note: Do not attempt salvage touchdown under uncertainty.

items.label: Trap: late descent and high energy near terminal area
items.value: Use controlled increased descent within limits and maintain speed discipline; go around if unstable in circuit.
items.note: Avoid abrupt idle dives and chase behavior.

items.label: Trap: MP-equipped assumptions
items.value: Use AFM-approved MP/RPM combinations only; expect reduced available MP at altitude.

items.label: Nightmare mode trigger phrase
items.value: If unsure and unstable: power, stabilize, or go around.
items.note: Decision speed is a safety skill.

items.label: Authority note
items.value: These are training heuristics, not dispatch authority.
items.note: AFM/POH + local SOP always override this guide.

sections.title: Nightmare Mode 2 - Close Distractor Decision Rules

items.label: Rule 1: break ties by margin
items.value: When two answers seem good, pick the one that restores airspeed/energy/stability earliest.

items.label: Rule 2: trend over snapshot
items.value: A 'currently acceptable' value is not enough if trend is diverging near the ground.
items.note: If trend worsens, trigger go-around sooner.

items.label: Rule 3: avoid coupled hazards
items.value: Reject options that combine high bank, high pitch, and low speed even if track looks better.
items.note: Do not trade margin for geometry.

items.label: Rule 4: power changes must be disciplined
items.value: Prefer small deliberate throttle adjustments with trim over large oscillatory corrections.

items.label: Rule 5: communication sequencing
items.value: In high-workload low-altitude moments, aviate first, then navigate, then communicate.
items.note: Radio delay is acceptable; margin loss is not.

items.label: Rule 6: MP-equipped nuance
items.value: Use approved AFM MP/RPM logic and environmental context; avoid hard-coded manifold assumptions.

items.label: Nightmare 2 cue
items.value: If two options feel equally 'reasonable,' choose the one that buys time and controllability.

items.label: Engine failure in flight
items.value: Aviate (best glide), navigate (landing area), communicate (when able)
items.note: Execute restart/secure checklist only after aircraft control.

items.label: Emergency landing (off-field)
items.value: Select field, plan high-key/low-key style path, secure cockpit before touchdown
items.note: Passenger brief and harness checks are mandatory.

items.label: Engine fire during start/ground
items.value: Continue cranking if directed by checklist, fuel/ignition actions per AFM
items.note: Evacuate and extinguish if fire persists.

items.label: In-flight fire or electrical smoke
items.value: Isolate source, switch off non-essential electrics, prepare immediate landing

items.label: Next content
items.value: Populate V-speeds, mass/balance limits, and wind component limits.
items.note: Keep values synced with your aircraft-specific AFM revision.

items.label: Next content
items.value: Add fuel, electrical and engine system quick notes for abnormal handling.

items.label: Next content
items.value: Add crosswind technique reminders, density altitude cues and go/no-go prompts.

items.label: Next content
items.value: Link each phase with the exact AFM checklist names used in training.
items.note: This guide supplements but does not replace the AFM checklist.
